project.name: GroundAtlas
project.summary: Open-source CLI-first repository context control plane that generates source-grounded maps, truth routes, and freshness gates for humans and AI agents without becoming a second source of truth.
goals: Generate deterministic source-grounded maps over repository truth surfaces.
Expose a fact-scoped SSOT model and orientation route for humans and AI agents.
Recognize vendor-neutral machine project manifests and ecosystem-specific manifest adapters without making SylphxAI Doctrine a public requirement.
Expose public guides, schema, examples, and a static landing page for open-source adoption.
Dogfood the vendor-neutral project.manifest.json control file in this repository.
Help humans and AI agents enter repositories faster while preserving canonical source ownership.
Provide safe CLI operations for init, update, scan, audit, explain, and impact analysis.
Detect stale generated maps through non-secret source fingerprints.
Expose machine-readable outputs suitable for CI gates, fleet scorecards, and future agent/MCP integrations.
Expose typed library exports for scanner, audit, explain, impact, fleet, and rendering primitives.
Publish the package to npm through CI-owned provenance and registry readback.
Dogfood GroundAtlas on its own repository in every required check.
Use evidence from public developer-tool ecosystems before extracting a standalone project-manifest/control-plane library.
Report adopted, warning, and blocked dogfooding status across local repository checkouts.
Run bounded source-checkout dogfood pilots against copied target repositories before claiming npm-based fleet adoption.
Publish the static landing page through GitHub Pages with least-privilege workflow permissions and no repository secrets.
Validate neutral project manifests in fleet dogfooding gates while reporting ecosystem manifests as adapters.
Expose a reusable GitHub Action gate for downstream update/audit/fleet adoption after npm publish and version-tag readback.
Expose read-only standalone manifest validation through the CLI and a public package subpath without making GroundAtlas the manifest SSOT.
Emit manifest and fleet JSON reports from the GitHub Action gate so downstream CI can persist adoption evidence as artifacts.
nonGoals: Own or replace downstream project truth such as ADRs, specs, schemas, tests, manifests, workflows, release proof, or source code.
Mutate source files or root agent prompts in scanned repositories.
Require LLM/provider credentials, hosted tracing, or remote state in the deterministic scan/audit path.
Publish packages manually from a workstation.

owns.name: groundatlas-cli
owns.description: CLI commands and deterministic scanner/renderer behavior for source-grounded repository maps.

owns.name: generated-map-format
owns.description: Machine-readable atlas JSON and generated Markdown navigation files.

owns.name: groundatlas-audit-gate
owns.description: Local audit rules that verify generated maps exist and declare their non-SSOT boundary.

owns.name: truth-orientation-model
owns.description: Fact-scoped SSOT model and read-order route for agents and humans entering a repository.

owns.name: freshness-audit-fingerprint
owns.description: Non-secret source fingerprint comparison that fails stale generated maps.

owns.name: manifest-validator-source
owns.description: Read-only manifest validation source for the CLI, library subpath, and exported schema bridge.

doesNotOwn: Downstream repository architecture, API, persistence, deployment, or release decisions.
Enterprise doctrine rules owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.
npm organization credentials, trusted-publishing settings, and registry administration outside the repository release workflow.

allowedDependencies.surface: local SylphxAI adapter, engineering doctrine, project manifest schema, and standards routing

forbiddenCouplings: Generated maps must never become canonical project truth.
GroundAtlas must not require private Sylphx infrastructure to run as an open-source CLI.
GroundAtlas must not mutate scanned source files or root agent prompts.
Future LLM integrations must remain optional adapters over deterministic source manifests.

runbooks.path: README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, docs/runbooks/publishing.md, docs/guides/, docs/website/

delivery.deployPath: Source changes are delivered by GitHub PR/CI. npm packages are published only by the tag-gated .github/workflows/release.yml workflow with provenance, registry readback, post-publish dogfood, and release evidence artifacts.
delivery.productionProof: Clean checkout runs bun install and bun run check successfully; generated-map audit includes freshness/non-SSOT proof; package release proof is npm registry readback for groundatlas@0.1.3 plus clean installed-package smoke and post-publish dogfood evidence. Public docs/schema/site proof requires validate:public-assets.

packageRelease.releaseIntent: Changesets-equivalent protected version PR updates package.json, action.yml defaults, changelog, and release evidence gates before a v*.*.* tag publish.
packageRelease.versionPr: Protected GitHub PR path today; future sylphx-release-bot/GitHub App version PR automation is the target before release scale-out.
packageRelease.publisher: Tag-gated GitHub Actions workflow publishes with npm provenance using a bounded NPM_TOKEN fallback while npm trusted publishing/OIDC setup remains open.
packageRelease.publishProof: npm registry readback, gitHead match, provenance publish, installed-package smoke, post-publish dogfood JSON, and groundatlas-release-evidence artifact.

gaps.description: Migrate future npm publishes from the bounded NPM_TOKEN fallback to npm trusted publishing/OIDC after npm package settings are configured and a tokenless release path is proven.
gaps.target: Before treating the token fallback as permanent release infrastructure

gaps.description: Add package-based GroundAtlas GitHub Action gates to downstream repositories, starting with Doctrine, before claiming mandatory fleet adoption.
gaps.owner: GroundAtlas maintainers and downstream repo owners
gaps.target: After groundatlas@0.1.3 registry readback and local Doctrine package pilot
